volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: The Women of Trachis (Section 1 - Section 184)

sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: λόγος μέν ἐστʼ ἀρχαῖος ἀνθρώπων φανείς ,
sections.translation: There is a saying among men , put forth long ago , that you cannot judge a mortal ’ s life and know whether it is good or bad until he dies . But well I know , even before I have passed away to Hades ’ domain , that my life is ill-fortuned and heavy .

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: ἔξοιδʼ ἔχουσα δυστυχῆ τε καὶ βαρύν ,
sections.translation: For I , while still dwelling in the house of my father Oeneus at Pleuron , had such fear of marriage as never any woman of Aetolia had . For my suitor was a river-god , Achelous ,

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: ὅς μʼ ἐν τρισὶν μορφαῖσιν ἐξῄτει πατρός ,
sections.translation: who in three shapes was always asking me from my father — coming now as a bull in visible form , now as a serpent , sheeny and coiled , now ox-faced with human trunk , while from his thick-shaded beard wellheads of fountain-water sprayed .

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: τοιόνδʼ ἐγὼ μνηστῆρα προσδεδεγμένη
sections.translation:
In the expectation that such a suitor would get me , I was always praying in my misery that I might die , before I should ever approach that marriage-bed .
But at last , to my joy , the glorious son of Zeus and Alcmena came and

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: ὃς εἰς ἀγῶνα τῷδε συμπεσὼν μάχης
sections.translation: closed with him in combat and delivered me . The manner of their fighting I cannot clearly recount . I know it not , but if there be anyone who watched that sight without trembling , he might give an account of it . But I , as I sat there , was struck with terror ,

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: μή μοι τὸ κάλλος ἄλγος ἐξεύροι ποτέ .
sections.translation: lest my beauty should win me sorrow in the end . But Zeus , Arbiter of Contests , accomplished a good ending — if indeed it was good . For since being joined with Heracles as his chosen bride , I nourish one fear after another in my anxiety for him . One night brings distress ,

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: καὶ νὺξ ἀπωθεῖ διαδεδεγμένη πόνον .
sections.translation: and the next night , in turn , drives it out . Children , furthermore , were born to us , whom at the time he looked at only as the farmer looks at a distant field , visiting it only once to sow seed and once to reap . Such was the manner of his life , ever sending him

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: αἰεὶ τὸν ἄνδρʼ ἔπεμπε λατρεύοντά τῳ .
sections.translation:
to and from our home in servitude to some master .
But now , when he has risen above those trials — now it is that my fear is keenest . For ever since he slew the mighty Iphitus , we have been dwelling here in Trachis , refugees

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: ξένῳ παρʼ ἀνδρὶ ναίομεν , κεῖνος δʼ ὅπου
sections.translation: in the home of a foreign host . But where Heracles is , no one knows . I only know that he is gone , and has caused me sharp pain for him . I am almost sure that he has come to some suffering . The interval has not been brief ; rather , he is unheard from ten months already ,

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: μῆνας πρὸς ἄλλοις πέντʼ ἀκήρυκτος μένει .
sections.translation: plus another five . Yes , there has been some terrible misfortune . That tablet which he left with me before departing suggests it to me . I often pray to the gods that I did not receive it to my misfortune .

sections.label: Section 49
sections.original: δέσποινα Δῃάνειρα , πολλὰ μέν σʼ ἐγὼ
sections.translation: Deianeira , my mistress , many are the times already that I have

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: κατεῖδον ἤδη πανδάκρυτʼ ὀδύρματα
sections.translation: observed you mourning the departure of Heracles with great weeping and lamentation . But now — if it is proper to school the free-born with the doctrines of a slave , and if I am obliged to point out what you should do — how is it that you so abound in sons , yet

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: ἀνδρὸς κατὰ ζήτησιν οὐ πέμπεις τινά ,
sections.translation: you do not send one of them to seek your husband , especially Hyllus ? It would be appropriate to send him , if he cared for his father ’ s sake that he be deemed to fare well . Here he is now , dashing to the house in timely stride . So if in your eyes I speak in season ,

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: πάρεστι χρῆσθαι τἀνδρὶ τοῖς τʼ ἐμοῖς λόγοις .
sections.translation: you can use both my counsel and the man .

sections.label: Section 61
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , ὦ παῖ , κἀξ ἀγεννήτων ἄρα
sections.translation: My child , my son , wise words fall , it seems , from humble lips . For this woman is a slave , but her advice is worthy of the free .

sections.label: Section 64
sections.original: ποῖον ; δίδαξον , μῆτερ , εἰ διδακτά μοι .
sections.translation: What sort of advice , Mother ? Tell me , if I may be told .

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: σὲ πατρὸς οὕτω δαρὸν ἐξενωμένου
sections.translation: It brings shame , she says , that you have not sought to learn of your father and where he is , when he has been abroad for so long .

sections.label: Section 67
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οἶδα , μύθοις εἴ τι πιστεύειν χρεών .
sections.translation: But I know where , if one can put any trust in rumors .

sections.label: Section 68
sections.original: καὶ ποῦ κλύεις νιν , τέκνον , ἱδρῦσθαι χθονός ;
sections.translation: And in what region , my child , do you hear that he has settled ?

sections.label: Section 69
sections.original: τὸν μὲν παρελθόντʼ ἄροτον ἐν μήκει χρόνου
sections.translation: Last year , they say , through all its length ,

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: Λυδῇ γυναικί φασί νιν λάτριν πονεῖν .
sections.translation: he toiled as a slave to a Lydian woman .

sections.label: Section 71
sections.original: πᾶν τοίνυν , εἰ καὶ τοῦτʼ ἔτλη , κλύοι τις ἄν .
sections.translation: If he endured even that , then one might believe any and all rumors .

sections.label: Section 72
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐξαφεῖται τοῦδέ γʼ , ὡς ἐγὼ κλύω .
sections.translation: Well , he has been released from that service , as I hear .

sections.label: Section 73
sections.original: ποῦ δῆτα νῦν ζῶν ἢ θανὼν ἀγγέλλεται ;
sections.translation: Where , then , is he reported to be now — alive , or dead ?

sections.label: Section 74
sections.original: Εὐβοΐδα χώραν φασίν , Εὐρύτου πόλιν ,
sections.translation: He is waging or yet planning a war ,

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: ἐπιστρατεύειν αὐτὸν ἢ μέλλειν ἔτι .
sections.translation: they say , upon Euboea , the realm of Eurytus .

sections.label: Section 76
sections.original: ἆρʼ οἶσθα δῆτʼ , ὦ τέκνον , ὡς ἔλειπέ μοι
sections.translation: Are you aware , my son , that he has left with me sure oracles concerning that land ?

sections.label: Section 78
sections.original: τὰ ποῖα , μῆτερ ; τὸν λόγον γὰρ ἀγνοῶ .
sections.translation: What are they , mother ? I do not know the oracles you mean .

sections.label: Section 79
sections.original: ὡς ἢ τελευτὴν τοῦ βίου μέλλει τελεῖν
sections.translation: They read that either he shall meet the end of his life ,

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: ἢ τοῦτον ἄρας ἆθλον εἰς τό γʼ ὕστερον
sections.translation: or , after taking on this contest , he shall thereafter , at least , enjoy a happy life for its duration . And so , my child , when his fate is thus trembling in the balance , will you not go to assist him ? For we are saved ,

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: κείνου βίον σώσαντος , ἢ οἰχόμεσθʼ ἅμα ;
sections.translation: if he finds safety , or we perish along with him .

sections.label: Section 86
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἶμι , μῆτερ · εἰ δὲ θεσφάτων ἐγὼ
sections.translation: I will go , Mother . Had I known the substance of these prophecies , I would have long been at his side . As it was , my father ’ s usual good fortune did not allow me to fear for him , nor to be overly anxious .

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: νῦν δʼ ὡς ξυνίημʼ , οὐδὲν ἐλλείψω τὸ μὴ οὐ
sections.translation: Now that I have the knowledge , I will spare no pains to learn the whole truth in this matter .

sections.label: Section 92
sections.original: χώρει νυν , ὦ παῖ · καὶ γὰρ ὑστέρῳ τό γʼ εὖ
sections.translation: Go , then , my son . For prosperity yields advantage even for him who learns of it late . Exeunt Hyllus , on one side , and the Nurse into the house .

sections.label: Section 1.94
sections.original: ὃν αἰόλα νὺξ ἐναριζομένα
sections.translation: You , to whom Night gives birth when she is vanquished and despoiled of her starry crown ,

sections.label: Section 1.95
sections.original: τίκτει κατευνάζει τε , φλογιζόμενον
sections.translation: and whom , as you blaze , she lays to rest , I pray you , O Sun , Sun , tell me , where is Alcmena ’ s son , where dwells her child ? O Shining god with your bright flash ,

sections.label: Section 1.100
sections.original: ἢ ποντίας αὐλῶνος ἢ δισσαῖσιν ἀπείροις κλιθείς ,
sections.translation: is he on the straits of the sea , or does he lean upon the twin continents ? Speak , you who surpass all in sight !

sections.label: Section 1.103
sections.original: ποθουμένᾳ γὰρ φρενὶ πυνθάνομαι
sections.translation: For with longing heart , as I hear , Deianeira , the battle-prize , now ,

sections.label: Section 1.105
sections.original: οἷά τινʼ ἄθλιον ὄρνιν ,
sections.translation: like some mournful bird , never rests her eyes ’ longing that they might be without tears , but nourishing a well-remembered fear for her husband ’ s travels she is constantly afflicted

sections.label: Section 1.110
sections.original: ἐνθυμίοις εὐναῖς ἀνανδρώτοισι τρύχεσθαι , κακὰν
sections.translation: by her anxious , widowed marriage-bed and in her misery anticipates misfortune .

sections.label: Section 2.112
sections.original: πολλὰ γὰρ ὥστʼ ἀκάμαντος ἢ νότου ἢ βορέα τις
sections.translation: For just as one may see billow after billow

sections.label: Section 2.115
sections.original: βιότου πολύπονον ὥσπερ πέλαγος
sections.translation: advancing and passing over the wide deep before the tireless south-wind , or the north , so the great toil of his life , stormy as the Cretan sea , now whirls back the heir of Cadmus , now exalts him . But some god always

sections.label: Section 2.120
sections.original: Κρήσιον . ἀλλά τις θεῶν αἰὲν ἀναμπλάκητον Ἅιδα
sections.translation: keeps him unerring from the house of Hades .

sections.label: Section 2.122
sections.original: ὧν ἐπιμεμφομένα σʼ αἰδοῖα μέν , ἀντία δʼ οἴσω .
sections.translation: With respect I reproach you for your weeping , but still I will speak in dissent .

sections.label: Section 2.125
sections.original: φαμὶ γὰρ οὐκ ἀποτρύειν ἐλπίδα τὰν ἀγαθὰν
sections.translation: You must not , I say , wear away fair hope . Remember that the all-accomplishing king , the son of Cronus , does not appoint a painless lot for mortals . Sorrow and joy revolve to all , as the stars of the Bear

sections.label: Section 2.130
sections.original: ἄρκτου στροφάδες κέλευθοι .
sections.translation: move in their circling paths .

sections.label: Section 132
sections.original: νὺξ βροτοῖσιν οὔτε κῆρες
sections.translation: Starry night does not remain constant with men , nor does tribulation , nor wealth ; in a moment it is gone from us , and to another in his turn come both

sections.label: Section 135
sections.original: χαίρειν τε καὶ στέρεσθαι .
sections.translation: gladness and bereavement . So I urge even you , our Queen , to keep these matters firmly in your outlook . You must , for who has

sections.label: Section 140
sections.original: τέκνοισι Ζῆνʼ ἄβουλον εἶδεν ;
sections.translation: known Zeus to be so without care for his children ?

sections.label: Section 141
sections.original: πεπυσμένη μέν , ὡς ἀπεικάσαι , πάρει
sections.translation: You have heard of my trouble , I would guess , and that has brought you here . But the anguish which consumes my heart — may you never come to know it through your own experience !— to that now you are total strangers . Yes , a young life grows in those sheltered regions

sections.label: Section 145
sections.original: χώροισιν αὑτοῦ , καί νιν οὐ θάλπος θεοῦ
sections.translation: of its own , and the Sun-god ’ s heat disturbs it not , nor rain , nor any wind . Rather it takes up a toilless existence amidst pleasure , until such time as she is called “ wife ” instead of “ maiden ”, and takes her portion of anxious thoughts in the night ,

sections.label: Section 150
sections.original: ἤτοι πρὸς ἀνδρὸς ἢ τέκνων φοβουμένη .
sections.translation:
fearing for husband or for children . At that point a woman could understand the burden of my misfortunes by recalling her own experience .
As a matter of fact , I have had many a trouble to weep for , but I am going to speak of one that none of the previous could equal .

sections.label: Section 155
sections.original: ὁδὸν γὰρ ἦμος τὴν τελευταίαν ἄναξ
sections.translation: When lord Heracles was setting out from home on his last journey , he left in the house an ancient tablet , inscribed with signs which he had never before brought himself to explain to me when going out on one of his many labors .

sections.label: Section 160
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὥς τι δράσων εἷρπε κοὐ θανούμενος .
sections.translation: He had always departed as if to conquer , not to die . But now , as if he were a doomed man , he told me what I should take for my marriage portion , and what share of their father ’ s land he wished divided for his children . And he fixed the time for the division , saying that , when he had been gone

sections.label: Section 165
sections.original: χώρας ἀπείη κἀνιαύσιον βεβώς ,
sections.translation: from our land for a year and three months , he was fated either to die at that time , or by escaping the end of the period to live thereafter an untroubled life . That , he explained , was the fate ordained by the gods

sections.label: Section 170
sections.original: τῶν Ἡρακλείων ἐκτελευτᾶσθαι πόνων ,
sections.translation: to be the end of the labors of Heracles just as , he said , the ancient oak at Dodona had once told him through the mouths of the two Peleiades . And it is in the present time that the truth of these prophecies is coming to pass , so that they must be fulfilled .

sections.label: Section 175
sections.original: ὥσθʼ ἡδέως εὕδουσαν ἐκπηδᾶν ἐμὲ
sections.translation: As a result I leap up from sweet sleep in fear , dear maidens , terrified at the possibility that I must remain widowed of the noblest man of all .

sections.label: Section 178
sections.original: εὐφημίαν νῦν ἴσχʼ · ἐπεὶ καταστεφῆ
sections.translation: Hush — no more ill-omened words ! I see a man approaching who is crowned with garlands as if for joyous news .

sections.label: Section 180
sections.original: δέσποινα Δῃάνειρα , πρῶτος ἀγγέλων
sections.translation: Queen Deianeira , I shall be the first messenger to free you from fear . Know that Alcmena ’ s son lives and triumphs , and from battle brings the first-fruits to the gods of this land .

sections.label: Section 184
sections.original: τίνʼ εἶπας , ὦ γεραιέ , τόνδε μοι λόγον ;
sections.translation: What news is this , old man , that you give me ?
